agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation in a logistics/courier system. A user (sender or customer) requests to cancel a previously placed delivery order. This is a critical lifecycle event that intersects with courier dispatch, package handling, billing, and customer satisfaction. The business impact of cancellation varies dramatically depending on where the order sits in the fulfillment pipeline.","bounded_context":"Order Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Order","definition":"A request for courier pickup and delivery of a package from origin to destination, with an associated lifecycle state."},{"term":"Order Status","definition":"The current lifecycle state of an order (e.g., Placed, Confirmed, Assigned, PickedUp, InTransit, Delivered, Cancelled)."},{"term":"Cancellation","definition":"A user-initiated request to terminate an order before delivery is completed. Results in the order transitioning to a Cancelled state."},{"term":"Cancellation Window","definition":"The set of order statuses during which cancellation is permitted without requiring manual intervention or dispute resolution."},{"term":"Cancellation Fee","definition":"A charge applied to the user when cancellation occurs after the platform has incurred irrecoverable costs (e.g., courier already dispatched)."},{"term":"Refund","definition":"The return of payment to the user, full or partial, triggered by a successful cancellation."},{"term":"Courier","definition":"The driver or delivery agent assigned to fulfill the order."},{"term":"Dispatch","definition":"The act of assigning a courier to an order and sending them pickup instructions."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"An order may only be cancelled if it has NOT yet reached a terminal state (Delivered, Cancelled, or Failed).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A delivered package cannot be undelivered. A cancelled order cannot be cancelled again. Allowing transitions from terminal states corrupts order lifecycle integrity.","testable_assertion":"Given an order in status Delivered, Cancelled, or Failed, any cancellation request MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"An order in status Placed or Confirmed (pre-dispatch) may be cancelled with a full refund and no cancellation fee.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The zero-fee window is a business policy. Leadership could decide to always charge a processing fee. The refund amount is a policy lever.","testable_assertion":"Given an order in status Placed or Confirmed, when the user cancels, then the full order amount is refunded and no cancellation fee is applied."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"An order in status Assigned or PickedUp (post-dispatch, pre-delivery) may be cancelled but a cancellation fee applies and the refund is partial.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Fee structure and refund percentages are business policy decisions, not physical constraints.","testable_assertion":"Given an order in status Assigned or PickedUp, when the user cancels, then a cancellation fee is deducted from the refund."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"An order in status InTransit may NOT be cancelled through self-service; it requires support escalation.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Whether to allow in-transit cancellation via self-service is a policy choice. Some businesses allow it with higher fees. But the default safe policy is to block self-service cancellation once a package is physically moving.","testable_assertion":"Given an order in status InTransit, when the user attempts cancellation, then the request is rejected with a message directing them to customer support."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Only the user who placed the order (the order owner) may cancel it.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing arbitrary users to cancel others' orders is a data integrity and security violation. This is an authorization invariant.","testable_assertion":"Given a cancellation request from a user who is NOT the order owner, the request MUST be rejected with an authorization error."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A cancelled order MUST record the cancellation reason, timestamp, and initiator for audit purposes.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Audit trail integrity is non-negotiable. Without it, dispute resolution, financial reconciliation, and fraud detection break down.","testable_assertion":"Given a successful cancellation, the order record MUST contain a non-null cancellation reason, a cancellation timestamp, and the identity of the cancelling user."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"When a dispatched order is cancelled, the assigned courier MUST be notified immediately.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"If the courier is not notified, they will continue to the pickup/delivery location, wasting time and fuel. This is an operational integrity constraint — the system state and the real world must stay in sync.","testable_assertion":"Given an order in status Assigned or PickedUp is cancelled, a notification event MUST be emitted to the assigned courier within the same transaction or saga."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"Cancellation MUST be idempotent — repeated cancellation requests for an already-cancelled order return success without side effects.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Without idempotency, network retries or user double-clicks could trigger duplicate refunds or duplicate fee charges, corrupting financial state.","testable_assertion":"Given an order already in Cancelled status, when the user submits another cancellation request, then the system returns success with no additional refund or fee processing."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An order in status Placed or Confirmed","when":"The order owner requests cancellation with a reason","then":"The order transitions to Cancelled, a full refund is issued, no fee is charged, the cancellation reason and timestamp are recorded, and the user receives confirmation."},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An order in status Assigned (courier dispatched but not yet picked up)","when":"The order owner requests cancellation","then":"The order transitions to Cancelled, a partial refund (minus cancellation fee) is issued, the courier is notified to stand down, and the cancellation is recorded with reason and timestamp."},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An order in status PickedUp (courier has the package)","when":"The order owner requests cancellation","then":"The order transitions to Cancelled, a partial refund (minus cancellation fee) is issued, the courier is notified to return or dispose of the package per policy, and the cancellation is recorded."},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An order in status InTransit","when":"The order owner requests cancellation via self-service","then":"The cancellation is rejected with HTTP 409 or equivalent conflict status, and the response directs the user to contact customer support."},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An order in status Delivered, Cancelled, or Failed","when":"Any user requests cancellation","then":"The cancellation is rejected with a clear error message stating the order is in a terminal state and cannot be cancelled."},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A user who did NOT place the order","when":"They attempt to cancel the order","then":"The request is rejected with HTTP 403 Forbidden, and no state change occurs on the order."},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An order that has already been cancelled","when":"The order owner submits another cancellation request","then":"The system returns a success response, no duplicate refund is issued, no additional fee is charged, and no duplicate notifications are sent to the courier."},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A cancellation request with no reason provided","when":"The system receives the request","then":"The request is rejected with a validation error requiring a cancellation reason."},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A successful cancellation of a dispatched order","when":"The cancellation is committed","then":"A courier notification event is emitted containing the order ID, cancellation reason, and any return-package instructions."},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A successful cancellation","when":"The order record is subsequently queried","then":"The record includes cancellation_reason (non-empty), cancelled_at (ISO 8601 timestamp), cancelled_by (user ID), and refund_amount."}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Cancellation request arrives at the exact moment the order transitions from Assigned to PickedUp (race condition).","expected_behavior":"The system must resolve the race atomically. The cancellation should either succeed against the state at the time of processing (with appropriate fee) or fail with a stale-state error prompting the user to retry. Optimistic locking or version checks on order state prevent split-brain."},{"scenario":"Courier picks up package, then user cancels — what happens to the physical package?","expected_behavior":"The courier must be notified with explicit return-to-sender or drop-at-nearest-depot instructions. The order record should track the package disposition. The cancellation fee must account for return logistics cost."},{"scenario":"Payment provider is unavailable when processing the refund during cancellation.","expected_behavior":"The order transitions to Cancelled immediately (state change is authoritative), and the refund is queued for async retry. The user is informed that the refund is being processed. A compensating mechanism retries the refund with exponential backoff. The order must NOT remain in a non-cancelled state just because the refund failed."},{"scenario":"User cancels an order, then contacts support claiming they didn't cancel.","expected_behavior":"The audit trail (cancelled_by, cancelled_at, cancellation_reason, IP/device metadata) provides evidence for dispute resolution. The system must not allow cancellation without authentication."},{"scenario":"Bulk cancellation — user attempts to cancel many orders simultaneously.","expected_behavior":"Each cancellation is processed independently. Partial failures are possible (some orders cancel, others reject). The response must clearly indicate per-order success/failure. Rate limiting should prevent abuse."},{"scenario":"Cancellation of an order with a promotional discount or coupon applied.","expected_behavior":"The refund amount must reflect the actual amount charged, not the pre-discount price. The coupon or promo code usage should be restored/credited back if business policy allows reuse."},{"scenario":"Network timeout — client sends cancellation but does not receive the response.","expected_behavior":"Idempotency (BR-8) ensures the client can safely retry. The second request returns success without double-processing the refund or fee."},{"scenario":"Order has multiple packages or legs (multi-stop delivery).","expected_behavior":"Cancellation applies to the entire order. Partial cancellation (cancel one leg) is a separate feature. If any leg is already delivered, the entire order cannot be fully cancelled via self-service."},{"scenario":"Cancellation requested by an admin or support agent on behalf of the user.","expected_behavior":"This is out of scope for this feature (user self-service cancellation). Admin cancellation should be a separate workflow with its own authorization and fee rules, but the cancelled_by field should distinguish between user-initiated and admin-initiated cancellations."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Cancellation fees offset sunk courier costs. Overly generous cancellation policies increase operational losses. Overly restrictive policies reduce customer lifetime value. Refund accuracy is critical — duplicate refunds are direct revenue loss. The cancellation fee structure directly impacts unit economics per order.","operational":"Courier notification on cancellation is critical for fleet efficiency. Unnotified couriers waste fuel, time, and capacity. The cancellation flow must integrate with dispatch, payment, and notification systems. Async refund processing adds operational complexity but is necessary for reliability.","user":"Cancellation is a trust-building feature. Users who can easily cancel feel safe placing orders. A clunky or opaque cancellation process drives users to competitors. Clear communication about fees, refund timelines, and status is essential. Blocking cancellation entirely (even when reasonable) creates support ticket volume.","risk":"Race conditions between cancellation and status transitions can lead to financial inconsistency (refund issued for a delivered package). Lack of idempotency risks duplicate refunds. Missing audit trails make fraud detection and dispute resolution impossible. Inconsistent courier notification can lead to real-world package mishandling.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"End Users (Senders)","impact":"Gain self-service cancellation capability. May incur fees for late cancellation. Expect clear refund timelines and confirmation."},{"group":"Couriers","impact":"Must receive timely cancellation notifications to avoid wasted trips. Need clear instructions on package disposition when cancellation occurs mid-fulfillment."},{"group":"Finance / Billing Team","impact":"Must reconcile cancellation fees and refunds. Need accurate audit trails. Duplicate refund prevention is critical."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Reduced ticket volume for simple cancellations. Will handle escalated InTransit cancellations and disputes. Need access to cancellation audit data."},{"group":"Operations / Dispatch","impact":"Cancelled-but-dispatched orders must be unwound. Courier availability must be restored in dispatch pool after cancellation notification."}]},"confidence":{"level":45,"reasoning":"The spec is a single sentence with no detail on order lifecycle states, fee structures, refund policies, courier notification requirements, or edge case handling. Every business rule and acceptance criterion above is inferred from general logistics domain knowledge. The actual system may have different states, different fee policies, or different cancellation windows. Without reviewing the codebase's actual Order entity, status enum, and event model, these are educated domain assumptions — not grounded in this system's ubiquitous language.","high_confidence_areas":["Terminal states cannot be cancelled (universal invariant)","Only the order owner may cancel (authorization invariant)","Audit trail is required for cancellations","Courier must be notified when dispatched orders are cancelled","Idempotency is required for cancellation requests"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact order status names and transitions in this system","Cancellation fee structure and amounts","Refund policy (full vs partial, timing)","Whether InTransit cancellation is blocked or allowed with escalation","Multi-package or multi-leg order handling","Promotional/coupon refund policy"],"assumptions":["The order lifecycle includes states similar to: Placed, Confirmed, Assigned, PickedUp, InTransit, Delivered, Cancelled, Failed","Payment is captured at order placement (not on delivery)","The system uses event-driven courier notifications","Cancellation fees exist as a concept in the business model","Self-service cancellation is the scope — admin/support cancellation is separate"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec does not define which order statuses permit cancellation. The cancellation window is the single most important business rule for this feature, and it is unspecified.","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Finance","Customer Support"],"options":["Allow cancellation in all pre-delivery states with varying fees","Allow cancellation only in pre-dispatch states","Allow cancellation in all non-terminal states with support escalation for late stages"],"recommendation":"Clarify with PM: provide the exact order states and for each state, whether cancellation is allowed, what fee applies, and what refund percentage is issued."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec does not define the cancellation fee structure. Is there a fee? Is it flat or percentage-based? Does it vary by cancellation timing?","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Finance"],"options":["No cancellation fee ever","Flat fee after dispatch","Percentage-based fee scaling with fulfillment progress"],"recommendation":"Clarify with PM and Finance: define the fee schedule tied to order status at time of cancellation."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec does not clarify who 'the user' is. Is this the sender, the recipient, or both? In logistics, sender and recipient may be different people with different cancellation rights.","affected_stakeholders":["End Users (Senders)","End Users (Recipients)"],"options":["Only the sender (order placer) can cancel","Both sender and recipient can cancel","Recipient can request cancellation but sender must approve"],"recommendation":"Clarify with PM: define which user role(s) have cancellation authority."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"No access to the codebase. Cannot verify actual Order entity, status enum, event model, or payment integration. All domain terms and states are inferred from general logistics knowledge, not from this system's ubiquitous language.","affected_stakeholders":["Development Team"],"options":["Provide codebase access for grounded analysis","Provide Order entity schema and status enum"],"recommendation":"Grant codebase access so acceptance criteria can be grounded in the system's actual domain model rather than assumed states."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation in a logistics/courier system. A user (sender or customer) requests to cancel a previously placed delivery order. This is a critical lifecycle event that intersects with courier dispatch, package handling, billing, and customer satisfaction. The business impact of cancellation varies dramatically depending on where the order sits in the fulfillment pipeline.","bounded_context":"Order Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Order","de
